item.text: /home/hatch/skills - what about this?

That's where the built-in Hatch skills live — Gmail, Google Calendar, Instagram, Spaces, etc. Each one has a `SKILL.md` I read when I need to use that integration.

`/home/hatch/workspace/skills/` is where I keep custom ones, like the IWSDK space deploy skill and the IWSDK dev skill.

parts.text: can you unzip and put this in skills?

item.text:
Done. Replaced the old IWSDK dev skill with your updated version:

```
workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/
├── SKILL.md
├── references/
│   ├── 01-scaffold-and-render.md
│   ├── 02-world-and-loop.md
│   ├── 03-subsystems.md
│   ├── 04-spatial-ui.md
│   ├── 05-verify-runtime.md
│   ├── 06-build-and-deploy.md
│   ├── 07-iterate.md
│   ├── api-quick-reference.md
│   ├── cli-reference.md
│   └── troubleshooting.md
└── scripts/
    └── preflight-checks.sh
```

10 reference docs, a preflight script, and the main SKILL.md — significantly more structured than the previous single-file version.

---
id: iwsdk-daily-build
enabled: true
mode: task
schedule:
  kind: daily
  timezone: America/Los_Angeles
  time: 00:00:00
timeout_secs: 25200
metadata:
  originating_channel_context_json: '{"originating_channel":"main","chat_kind":"direct","event_kind":"message","require_mention":false,"device_id":"435a7515-6e67-4449-ae9e-9f1df487a8ae"}'
---
## IWSDK Daily Build — Initial Planning & Build

This is the midnight kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md` — especially section **4b (Spatial UI / PanelUI)**.
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends**, update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**ALL game UI MUST use IWSDK's built-in PanelUI system. Do NOT use HTML DOM elements for any game UI.**

This is a hard gate. HTML `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, or any DOM overlay for menus, HUDs, scorecards, settings, notifications, or any other game UI is **forbidden**. HTML overlays are invisible in XR and are an anti-pattern.

Instead, use:
- **`.uikitml` templates** in a `ui/` directory, compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- **`PanelUI` component** to attach compiled UI panels to entities
- **`Follower` component** with `world.player.head` target for head-locked HUDs in XR
- **`ScreenSpace` component** for browser-mode overlays that auto-return to world-space in XR
- **World-space panels** for in-scene menus, leaderboards, settings boards

Read the IWSDK dev skill section **4b** for full API details, examples, and patterns.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features or adding content.**

Build order for every game:
1. Core gameplay loop + basic XR controller input
2. All UI as PanelUI spatial panels (menus, HUD, scorecards, settings)
3. **VERIFY in XR:** Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
   - All panels render and are readable in XR
   - XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
   - HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
   - No HTML overlays remain
4. ONLY THEN expand with additional features, content, modes, polish

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **ALL UI via PanelUI** — `.uikitml` templates, `Follower` for HUDs, `ScreenSpace` for overlays. Zero HTML DOM UI.
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode with dual runtime (`xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `browserControls: true`)
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build.

- Set `last_updated_utc`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**ALL game UI MUST use IWSDK's built-in PanelUI system. Do NOT use HTML DOM elements for any game UI.**

This is a hard gate. HTML `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, or any DOM overlay for menus, HUDs, scorecards, settings, notifications, or any other game UI is **forbidden**. HTML overlays are invisible in XR and are an anti-pattern.

Instead, use:
- **`.uikitml` templates** in a `ui/` directory, compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- **`PanelUI` component** to attach compiled UI panels to entities
- **`Follower` component** with `world.player.head` target for head-locked HUDs in XR
- **`ScreenSpace` component** for browser-mode overlays that auto-return to world-space in XR
- **World-space panels** for in-scene menus, leaderboards, settings boards

Read the IWSDK dev skill section **4b** for full API details, examples, and patterns.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features or adding content.**

Build order for every game:
1. Core gameplay loop + basic XR controller input
2. All UI as PanelUI spatial panels (menus, HUD, scorecards, settings)
3. **VERIFY in XR:** Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
   - All panels render and are readable in XR
   - XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
   - HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
   - No HTML overlays remain
4. ONLY THEN expand with additional features, content, modes, polish

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **ALL UI via PanelUI** — `.uikitml` templates, `Follower` for HUDs, `ScreenSpace` for overlays. Zero HTML DOM UI.
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode with dual runtime (`xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `browserControls: true`)
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build.

item.output:
---
name: iwsdk-dev
description: >-
  MUST read BEFORE building VR/MR or browser-first 3D apps with IWSDK (Immersive
  Web SDK / @iwsdk/core). A phase-based build guide — scaffold → world+loop →
  subsystems → spatial UI → verify → deploy → iterate — that keeps the
  non-negotiable correctness rules always in view and loads a focused reference
  per phase. Use when scaffolding, building, debugging, or deploying WebXR/3D
  apps, when working with PanelUI/uikitml, ECS systems, physics, the iwsdk CLI,
  or when the user mentions iwsdk, IWSDK, @iwsdk, WebXR, or `npx iwsdk`.
---

# IWSDK Development Guide

Targets the IWSDK **0.4.x** line · [iwsdk.dev](https://iwsdk.dev) · built on Three.js (super-three) + an ECS.

This skill is phase-based. Find the phase you're in below, read its terse highlights here,
and **load the one matching `references/` file** for depth. The correctness rules in the next
section are always in force, in every phase.

## CLI-first operating model

`npx iwsdk ...` is the primary control surface in cloud/headless harnesses — for setup, dev
server, screenshots, ECS/XR inspection, and **reference lookup**. Prefer it over MCP tools,
which are often unwired in cloud harnesses; do not rewrite CLI examples into MCP-only flows.

**Ground your API knowledge in the reference CLI instead of guessing.** `@iwsdk/reference` is a
semantic search over the real SDK (`reference search` / `api` / `systems` / `components` /
`examples`). It needs a **one-time warmup** that downloads a corpus + model into cache:

```bash
npx iwsdk reference warmup     # one-time, ~120MB into cache; run it during scaffold (Phase 0)
npx iwsdk reference status      # confirm ready
npx iwsdk reference api    --input-json '{"name":"World.create"}'
npx iwsdk reference search --input-json '{"query":"how to register a per-frame system","limit":5}'
```

> **If a reference command errors as not-ready / cache-miss / corpus-missing, RUN
> `npx iwsdk reference warmup` and retry — do NOT give up and guess the API.** Warmup is cheap
> and pays for itself: it is your ground truth for any "what's the real method/component?"
> question and the antidote to the `(world as any)` / wrong-API guessing that breaks builds.
> (See `references/cli-reference.md` for warmup recovery, and `references/api-quick-reference.md`
> for the typed surface at a glance.)

## The 7 build phases

| Phase                       | You are…                                     | Load                                   |
| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| 0 · Scaffold & First Render | creating the app + getting a frame to render | `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md` |
| 1 · World & the Game Loop   | calling `World.create`, standing up the loop | `references/02-world-and-loop.md`      |
| 2 · Gameplay Subsystems     | three imports, physics, audio, input, coords | `references/03-subsystems.md`          |
| 3 · Spatial UI (PanelUI)    | authoring + wiring uikitml panels            | `references/04-spatial-ui.md`          |
| 4 · Verify (continuous)     | proving it renders, ticks, responds          | `references/05-verify-runtime.md`      |
| 5 · Build & Deploy          | deploying — Hatch Space (default), GH Pages opt-in | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md`    |
| 6 · Iterate                 | re-verifying + extending across rounds       | `references/07-iterate.md`             |

## Non-negotiable correctness rules (always in force)

These six prevent the most expensive failures. Each is _decided early but surfaces late_, so
internalize them up front. Terse here; full treatment in the tagged reference.

1. **Import every Three.js class from `@iwsdk/core`, never from `three`.** The runtime is
   super-three; importing from `three` (or `import * as THREE`) drifts types and `instanceof`.
   IWSDK apps are ESM-only — no `require`. _[Phase 2 → 03-subsystems.md]_
2. **Per-frame logic MUST be an ECS System** — `class X extends createSystem({...})` with
   `update(delta, time)`, registered via `world.registerSystem(X)`. `world.onUpdate`,
   `world.ecs.registerSystem`, `renderer.setAnimationLoop`, and raw `requestAnimationFrame`
   do **not** tick the loop and silently ship a dead game. _[Phase 1 → 02-world-and-loop.md]_
3. **`World` is fully typed — never `(world as any)`.** `world.camera`, `world.scene`,
   `world.renderer`, `world.input`, `world.player`, `world.registerSystem` all exist. A TS error
   here is a real bug, not a framework quirk. _[Phase 1 → 02-world-and-loop.md]_
4. **PanelUI loads async, is uikit (not DOM), and needs relative config paths.** Wire handlers
   in the query `'qualify'` event (not `setTimeout`); mutate with `setProperties` (not
   `textContent`/`onclick`); author `config: './ui/x.json'` relative (root-absolute breaks deploy: GitHub 404s, and the Space daemon serves only `assets/`).
   _[Phase 3 author → 04-spatial-ui.md · Phase 5 bites → 06-build-and-deploy.md]_
5. **`tsc --noEmit` + a rendered screenshot is the real verification gate.** `vite build` does
   not type-check; a green build is **not** verification. Re-run the gate every milestone.
   _[Phase 4 → 05-verify-runtime.md]_
6. **Use the real subsystem APIs, not hand-rolled substitutes.** Physics needs `PhysicsShape` +
   `PhysicsBody` (don't write an integrator); audio uses `AudioSource` / `AudioUtils` (not raw
   Web Audio). _[Phase 2 → 03-subsystems.md]_

When unsure of any API, confirm it with `npx iwsdk reference api|systems|components` (warm it up
first) rather than casting to `any`.

## Phase highlights

### Phase 0 — Scaffold & First Render → `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md`

- Check `node -v` ≥ 20.19 **before** scaffolding (Vite 7 throws `crypto.hash is not a function`
  on Node 18; `dev:runtime` spawns a child `vite` that reverts to the default Node — make 20/22
  the persistent default, don't drop it in `/tmp`).
- Scaffold **fresh** with `npx @iwsdk/create@latest … --no-metaspatial`; infer flags from the
  request. Never clone-and-rename a prior game (drags stale assets/config/anti-patterns).
- `npm run dev` == `npx iwsdk dev up`; read `localUrl` (`https://127.0.0.1:8081/`) from
  `dev status`. SwiftShader auto-detects in 0.4.x — no `node_modules` patching.
- **Warm up the reference CLI now:** `npx iwsdk reference warmup` (so API lookups work later).
- Behind a proxy / on Node-18 / on a Playwright `chromium_headless_shell-1208` mismatch → load
  the reference.

### Phase 1 — World & the Game Loop → `references/02-world-and-loop.md`

- `await World.create(container, {...})`; options are typed (don't cast). Rules **2 and 3** live
  here — this is the costly correctness fork.
- After `World.create`, register systems: `world.registerSystem(GameSystem)`. The loop is
  already running; never start your own.

### Phase 2 — Gameplay Subsystems → `references/03-subsystems.md`

- Three imports (rule 1), physics components (rule 6), `AudioSource`/`AudioUtils` (rule 6),
  XR controller + keyboard input, coordinates. One file, jump via its `## Contents`.
- Input gamepad APIs take **string ids** (`InputComponent.Trigger`) and `getAxesValues` returns
  `{x,y}` — a number id or array index silently returns `undefined`.

### Phase 3 — Spatial UI (PanelUI / uikitml) → `references/04-spatial-ui.md`

- Author `ui/*.uikitml`; the `compileUIKit()` vite plugin emits `public/ui/*.json` (no CLI to
  compile). Reference the **compiled** path, **relative** (rule 4).
- Loads async → wire in the `'qualify'` event; elements are uikit → `setProperties`. ASCII-only
  glyphs; no multi-value `padding`/`margin` shorthand.

### Phase 4 — Verify in the Live Runtime → `references/05-verify-runtime.md`

- Rule 5. `npx tsc --noEmit` → `dev up` → `browser screenshot` → `browser logs` (zero errors) →
  `ecs systems` (your systems are registered = the loop is real). Re-run every milestone.
- `ecs pause`/`step` to freeze a play frame for a stable screenshot.

### Phase 5 — Build & Deploy → `references/06-build-and-deploy.md`

- `npm run build`, then **deploy as a Hatch Space by default**: `create_space` (registers it) →
  rewrite `index.html` (both `data-generated-space-root=""` + `data-generated-space-bootstrap=""`
  markers, drop the SDK bundle) → move every asset under `assets/` → patch runtime paths to
  `./assets/…` → `share share-space --slug <slug>`. Rebuild before publishing (stale-artifact trap).
- **GitHub Pages is opt-in** — only when the user explicitly asks (or needs VM-independent
  hosting, since a `local` Space needs the VM running): `npm run build && npx gh-pages -d dist`.
- The #1 deploy break: the Space daemon serves **only `assets/`**, so any runtime-fetched path
  (PanelUI `config`, gltf/audio/textures) 401s unless it resolves under `./assets/` — and the same
  root-absolute `config: '/ui/…'` 404s on a GitHub subpath. Author configs relative.

### Phase 6 — Iterate → `references/07-iterate.md`

- Re-run the full Phase 4 gate **every** round against _this_ build; never reuse a prior round's
  counts/claims. Recover an unresponsive runtime; re-sync adapters after dependency changes.

## When to load references (route by what you're doing)

| If you're… / you hit…                                                  | Load                                |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| "nothing is ticking / `ecs systems` is empty / dead loop"              | `references/02-world-and-loop.md`   |
| "a panel won't appear or bind"                                         | `references/04-spatial-ui.md`       |
| "401/404 on panels or assets **after deploy**" (Space or Pages)        | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md` |
| "build passed — is it done?"                                           | `references/05-verify-runtime.md`   |
| confirming a real member/signature (instead of casting to `any`)       | `references/api-quick-reference.md` |
| any `iwsdk` command, warmup recovery, or "does this subcommand exist?" | `references/cli-reference.md`       |
| a raw error string you can't place                                     | `references/troubleshooting.md`     |

Run the bundled gates with `bash scripts/preflight-checks.sh` (non-ASCII glyphs, Yoga shorthand,
root-absolute UI configs, CJS idioms, uikitml→json coverage) before deploying.

## Top friction points (most-wasteful mistakes, by phase)

1. Shipping a **no-op game loop** via raw rAF / `world.onUpdate` / `world.ecs.registerSystem` — _P1_.
2. Reinventing **PanelUI readiness** with `setTimeout` instead of the `'qualify'` event — _P3_.
3. **Assets the Space daemon won't serve** (everything fetched at runtime must live under `assets/`) / root-absolute UI config that 404s on a GitHub subpath — _P3/P5_.
4. Treating a green `vite build` as **verification** (it doesn't type-check) — _P4_.
5. Casting `(world as any)` / guessing APIs instead of warming up the **reference CLI** — _all phases_.
6. Building on **Node 18** (Vite 7 `crypto.hash`) or cloning a prior game as a scaffold — _P0_.

item.text:
The current prompts reference `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md` — which is the right path — but they say "especially section **4b (Spatial UI / PanelUI)**" which doesn't exist in the new skill. The new one is phase-based with separate reference docs. Here's the updated AM version:

---

```markdown
## IWSDK Daily Build — Initial Planning & Build

This is the midnight kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md` — internalize the 7 build phases and all 6 non-negotiable correctness rules before writing any code.
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends**, update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build phases — follow the skill

The IWSDK dev skill defines 7 phases. Follow them in order, loading the matching `references/` file at each phase:

| Phase | What | Reference to load |
|-------|------|-------------------|
| 0 | Scaffold & first render | `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md` |
| 1 | World & game loop | `references/02-world-and-loop.md` |
| 2 | Gameplay subsystems (physics, audio, input) | `references/03-subsystems.md` |
| 3 | Spatial UI (PanelUI / uikitml) | `references/04-spatial-ui.md` |
| 4 | Verify in live runtime | `references/05-verify-runtime.md` |
| 5 | Build & deploy | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md` |
| 6 | Iterate across rounds | `references/07-iterate.md` |

**Load each reference file as you enter that phase.** Don't front-load all of them.

When unsure of any API, use `npx iwsdk reference api|search|systems|components` — warm it up in Phase 0 with `npx iwsdk reference warmup`. Never cast `(world as any)` or guess.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

ALL game UI MUST use PanelUI. No HTML DOM elements (`<div>`, `<canvas>`, `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`) for any game UI — they are invisible in XR. Use `.uikitml` templates, `PanelUI` component, `Follower` for HUDs, `ScreenSpace` for browser-mode overlays. Full details in `references/04-spatial-ui.md`.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Verification Gate

`tsc --noEmit` + a rendered screenshot is the real gate — `vite build` does NOT type-check. Re-run every milestone: `tsc --noEmit` → `dev up` → `browser screenshot` → `browser logs` (zero errors) → `ecs systems` (your systems are registered). Full protocol in `references/05-verify-runtime.md`.

Run `bash ~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/scripts/preflight-checks.sh` before deploying to catch non-ASCII glyphs, Yoga shorthand, root-absolute UI configs, and CJS idioms.

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **ALL UI via PanelUI** — `.uikitml` templates, zero HTML DOM UI
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — use IWSDK's `InputComponent` API and action system. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode with dual runtime (`xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `browserControls: true`)
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build.
```

---

Key changes from the old version:
- References the phase-based structure instead of the nonexistent "section 4b"
- Adds the phase table so the builder knows to load one reference at a time
- Adds `npx iwsdk reference warmup` in Phase 0
- Adds `preflight-checks.sh` before deploy
- Removes duplicated correctness rules that are now in the skill itself

{"action":"spawn_agent","message":"Build a **rocket launch simulation** VR game using IWSDK 0.4.1+.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md` — internalize the 7 build phases and all 6 non-negotiable correctness rules before writing any code. Follow the phases in order, loading each `references/` file as you enter that phase.
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Game concept: Rocket Launch Simulation

A VR rocket launch simulation game. The player builds/configures a rocket, launches it, and controls/guides it through stages of flight. Think Kerbal Space Program meets holodeck aesthetic. Could include: stage separation, trajectory control, orbital mechanics (simplified), fuel management, mission objectives, different rocket configurations, launch pad environments, mission control HUD. Be creative with the concept but keep it grounded in the rocket launch theme.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "2026-06-09",
  "cycle": "pm",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends**, update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build phases — follow the skill

| Phase | What | Reference to load |
|-------|------|-------------------|
| 0 | Scaffold & first render | `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md` |
| 1 | World & game loop | `references/02-world-and-loop.md` |
| 2 | Gameplay subsystems (physics, audio, input) | `references/03-subsystems.md` |
| 3 | Spatial UI (PanelUI / uikitml) | `references/04-spatial-ui.md` |
| 4 | Verify in live runtime | `references/05-verify-runtime.md` |
| 5 | Build & deploy | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md` |
| 6 | Iterate across rounds | `references/07-iterate.md` |

**Load each reference file as you enter that phase.** Don't front-load all of them.

When unsure of any API, use `npx iwsdk reference api|search|systems|components` — warm it up in Phase 0 with `npx iwsdk reference warmup`. Never cast `(world as any)` or guess.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

ALL game UI MUST use PanelUI. No HTML DOM elements for any game UI — they are invisible in XR. Use `.uikitml` templates, `PanelUI` component, `Follower` for HUDs, `ScreenSpace` for browser-mode overlays.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Verification Gate

`tsc --noEmit` + a rendered screenshot is the real gate. Re-run every milestone. Run `bash ~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/scripts/preflight-checks.sh` before deploying.

### Build instructions:

1. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - ALL UI via PanelUI — zero HTML DOM UI
   - XR controller support mandatory — use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system
   - Dual runtime: `xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `browserControls: true`
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

2. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

3. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```

4. **Push intermediate commits** to main and redeploy at major milestones.

5. **When your round ends**, update the status file, the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md`.

6. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

Hourly PM sentinel check-in jobs (1pm–7pm PT) will monitor your status file and continue the build if you stop early."}

---
name: iwsdk-dev
description: >-
  MUST read BEFORE building VR/MR or browser-first 3D apps with IWSDK (Immersive
  Web SDK / @iwsdk/core). A phase-based build guide — scaffold → world+loop →
  subsystems → spatial UI → verify → deploy → iterate — that keeps the
  non-negotiable correctness rules always in view and loads a focused reference
  per phase. Use when scaffolding, building, debugging, or deploying WebXR/3D
  apps, when working with PanelUI/uikitml, ECS systems, physics, the iwsdk CLI,
  or when the user mentions iwsdk, IWSDK, @iwsdk, WebXR, or `npx iwsdk`.
---

# IWSDK Development Guide

Targets the IWSDK **0.4.x** line · [iwsdk.dev](https://iwsdk.dev) · built on Three.js (super-three) + an ECS.

This skill is phase-based. Find the phase you're in below, read its terse highlights here,
and **load the one matching `references/` file** for depth. The correctness rules in the next
section are always in force, in every phase.

## CLI-first operating model

`npx iwsdk ...` is the primary control surface in cloud/headless harnesses — for setup, dev
server, screenshots, ECS/XR inspection, and **reference lookup**. Prefer it over MCP tools,
which are often unwired in cloud harnesses; do not rewrite CLI examples into MCP-only flows.

**Ground your API knowledge in the reference CLI instead of guessing.** `@iwsdk/reference` is a
semantic search over the real SDK (`reference search` / `api` / `systems` / `components` /
`examples`). It needs a **one-time warmup** that downloads a corpus + model into cache:

```bash
npx iwsdk reference warmup     # one-time, ~120MB into cache; run it during scaffold (Phase 0)
npx iwsdk reference status      # confirm ready
npx iwsdk reference api    --input-json '{"name":"World.create"}'
npx iwsdk reference search --input-json '{"query":"how to register a per-frame system","limit":5}'
```

> **If a reference command errors as not-ready / cache-miss / corpus-missing, RUN
> `npx iwsdk reference warmup` and retry — do NOT give up and guess the API.** Warmup is cheap
> and pays for itself: it is your ground truth for any "what's the real method/component?"
> question and the antidote to the `(world as any)` / wrong-API guessing that breaks builds.
> (See `references/cli-reference.md` for warmup recovery, and `references/api-quick-reference.md`
> for the typed surface at a glance.)

## The 7 build phases

| Phase                       | You are…                                     | Load                                   |
| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| 0 · Scaffold & First Render | creating the app + getting a frame to render | `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md` |
| 1 · World & the Game Loop   | calling `World.create`, standing up the loop | `references/02-world-and-loop.md`      |
| 2 · Gameplay Subsystems     | three imports, physics, audio, input, coords | `references/03-subsystems.md`          |
| 3 · Spatial UI (PanelUI)    | authoring + wiring uikitml panels            | `references/04-spatial-ui.md`          |
| 4 · Verify (continuous)     | proving it renders, ticks, responds          | `references/05-verify-runtime.md`      |
| 5 · Build & Deploy          | deploying — Hatch Space (default), GH Pages opt-in | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md`    |
| 6 · Iterate                 | re-verifying + extending across rounds       | `references/07-iterate.md`             |

## Non-negotiable correctness rules (always in force)

These six prevent the most expensive failures. Each is _decided early but surfaces late_, so
internalize them up front. Terse here; full treatment in the tagged reference.

1. **Import every Three.js class from `@iwsdk/core`, never from `three`.** The runtime is
   super-three; importing from `three` (or `import * as THREE`) drifts types and `instanceof`.
   IWSDK apps are ESM-only — no `require`. _[Phase 2 → 03-subsystems.md]_
2. **Per-frame logic MUST be an ECS System** — `class X extends createSystem({...})` with
   `update(delta, time)`, registered via `world.registerSystem(X)`. `world.onUpdate`,
   `world.ecs.registerSystem`, `renderer.setAnimationLoop`, and raw `requestAnimationFrame`
   do **not** tick the loop and silently ship a dead game. _[Phase 1 → 02-world-and-loop.md]_
3. **`World` is fully typed — never `(world as any)`.** `world.camera`, `world.scene`,
   `world.renderer`, `world.input`, `world.player`, `world.registerSystem` all exist. A TS error
   here is a real bug, not a framework quirk. _[Phase 1 → 02-world-and-loop.md]_
4. **PanelUI loads async, is uikit (not DOM), and needs relative config paths.** Wire handlers
   in the query `'qualify'` event (not `setTimeout`); mutate with `setProperties` (not
   `textContent`/`onclick`); author `config: './ui/x.json'` relative (root-absolute breaks deploy: GitHub 404s, and the Space daemon serves only `assets/`).
   _[Phase 3 author → 04-spatial-ui.md · Phase 5 bites → 06-build-and-deploy.md]_
5. **`tsc --noEmit` + a rendered screenshot is the real verification gate.** `vite build` does
   not type-check; a green build is **not** verification. Re-run the gate every milestone.
   _[Phase 4 → 05-verify-runtime.md]_
6. **Use the real subsystem APIs, not hand-rolled substitutes.** Physics needs `PhysicsShape` +
   `PhysicsBody` (don't write an integrator); audio uses `AudioSource` / `AudioUtils` (not raw
   Web Audio). _[Phase 2 → 03-subsystems.md]_

When unsure of any API, confirm it with `npx iwsdk reference api|systems|components` (warm it up
first) rather than casting to `any`.

## Phase highlights

### Phase 0 — Scaffold & First Render → `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md`

- Check `node -v` ≥ 20.19 **before** scaffolding (Vite 7 throws `crypto.hash is not a function`
  on Node 18; `dev:runtime` spawns a child `vite` that reverts to the default Node — make 20/22
  the persistent default, don't drop it in `/tmp`).
- Scaffold **fresh** with `npx @iwsdk/create@latest … --no-metaspatial`; infer flags from the
  request. Never clone-and-rename a prior game (drags stale assets/config/anti-patterns).
- `npm run dev` == `npx iwsdk dev up`; read `localUrl` (`https://127.0.0.1:8081/`) from
  `dev status`. SwiftShader auto-detects in 0.4.x — no `node_modules` patching.
- **Warm up the reference CLI now:** `npx iwsdk reference warmup` (so API lookups work later).
- Behind a proxy / on Node-18 / on a Playwright `chromium_headless_shell-1208` mismatch → load
  the reference.

### Phase 1 — World & the Game Loop → `references/02-world-and-loop.md`

- `await World.create(container, {...})`; options are typed (don't cast). Rules **2 and 3** live
  here — this is the costly correctness fork.
- After `World.create`, register systems: `world.registerSystem(GameSystem)`. The loop is
  already running; never start your own.

### Phase 2 — Gameplay Subsystems → `references/03-subsystems.md`

- Three imports (rule 1), physics components (rule 6), `AudioSource`/`AudioUtils` (rule 6),
  XR controller + keyboard input, coordinates. One file, jump via its `## Contents`.
- Input gamepad APIs take **string ids** (`InputComponent.Trigger`) and `getAxesValues` returns
  `{x,y}` — a number id or array index silently returns `undefined`.

### Phase 3 — Spatial UI (PanelUI / uikitml) → `references/04-spatial-ui.md`

- Author `ui/*.uikitml`; the `compileUIKit()` vite plugin emits `public/ui/*.json` (no CLI to
  compile). Reference the **compiled** path, **relative** (rule 4).
- Loads async → wire in the `'qualify'` event; elements are uikit → `setProperties`. ASCII-only
  glyphs; no multi-value `padding`/`margin` shorthand.

### Phase 4 — Verify in the Live Runtime → `references/05-verify-runtime.md`

- Rule 5. `npx tsc --noEmit` → `dev up` → `browser screenshot` → `browser logs` (zero errors) →
  `ecs systems` (your systems are registered = the loop is real). Re-run every milestone.
- `ecs pause`/`step` to freeze a play frame for a stable screenshot.

### Phase 5 — Build & Deploy → `references/06-build-and-deploy.md`

- `npm run build`, then **deploy as a Hatch Space by default**: `create_space` (registers it) →
  rewrite `index.html` (both `data-generated-space-root=""` + `data-generated-space-bootstrap=""`
  markers, drop the SDK bundle) → move every asset under `assets/` → patch runtime paths to
  `./assets/…` → `share share-space --slug <slug>`. Rebuild before publishing (stale-artifact trap).
- **GitHub Pages is opt-in** — only when the user explicitly asks (or needs VM-independent
  hosting, since a `local` Space needs the VM running): `npm run build && npx gh-pages -d dist`.
- The #1 deploy break: the Space daemon serves **only `assets/`**, so any runtime-fetched path
  (PanelUI `config`, gltf/audio/textures) 401s unless it resolves under `./assets/` — and the same
  root-absolute `config: '/ui/…'` 404s on a GitHub subpath. Author configs relative.

### Phase 6 — Iterate → `references/07-iterate.md`

- Re-run the full Phase 4 gate **every** round against _this_ build; never reuse a prior round's
  counts/claims. Recover an unresponsive runtime; re-sync adapters after dependency changes.

## When to load references (route by what you're doing)

| If you're… / you hit…                                                  | Load                                |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| "nothing is ticking / `ecs systems` is empty / dead loop"              | `references/02-world-and-loop.md`   |
| "a panel won't appear or bind"                                         | `references/04-spatial-ui.md`       |
| "401/404 on panels or assets **after deploy**" (Space or Pages)        | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md` |
| "build passed — is it done?"                                           | `references/05-verify-runtime.md`   |
| confirming a real member/signature (instead of casting to `any`)       | `references/api-quick-reference.md` |
| any `iwsdk` command, warmup recovery, or "does this subcommand exist?" | `references/cli-reference.md`       |
| a raw error string you can't place                                     | `references/troubleshooting.md`     |

Run the bundled gates with `bash scripts/preflight-checks.sh` (non-ASCII glyphs, Yoga shorthand,
root-absolute UI configs, CJS idioms, uikitml→json coverage) before deploying.

## Top friction points (most-wasteful mistakes, by phase)

1. Shipping a **no-op game loop** via raw rAF / `world.onUpdate` / `world.ecs.registerSystem` — _P1_.
2. Reinventing **PanelUI readiness** with `setTimeout` instead of the `'qualify'` event — _P3_.
3. **Assets the Space daemon won't serve** (everything fetched at runtime must live under `assets/`) / root-absolute UI config that 404s on a GitHub subpath — _P3/P5_.
4. Treating a green `vite build` as **verification** (it doesn't type-check) — _P4_.
5. Casting `(world as any)` / guessing APIs instead of warming up the **reference CLI** — _all phases_.
6. Building on **Node 18** (Vite 7 `crypto.hash`) or cloning a prior game as a scaffold — _P0_.

item.output:
# Phase 6 — Iterate (Debug & Extend Across Rounds)

Building an IWSDK app is a loop: change code, re-verify, extend, repeat. The
hazard is treating a later round like it inherits the last round's guarantees.
It does not. Every round runs against a _new_ build of a _new_ runtime — so the
verification gate, the API confirmations, and the recovery steps all have to run
again. This file is the cross-round discipline; it points at the other phase
files for the full procedures rather than restating them.

## Contents

- [The one rule that defines this phase](#the-one-rule-that-defines-this-phase)
- [Re-run the FULL verification gate every round](#re-run-the-full-verification-gate-every-round)
- [Recovering an unresponsive runtime](#recovering-an-unresponsive-runtime)
- [Re-sync adapters after dependency changes](#re-sync-adapters-after-dependency-changes)
- [Confirm APIs against the reference, never guess](#confirm-apis-against-the-reference-never-guess)
- [Round checklist](#round-checklist)

## The one rule that defines this phase

**Never copy entity/component counts or "verified" claims from a prior round.**
A note like "XR verification: 18 entities, 58 components" is only meaningful from
_this_ build's running runtime. Counts from a previous game's notes, a
`BUILD_LOG`, or an earlier round of the same game say nothing about what the
current code does. Re-derive them against THIS build:

```bash
npx iwsdk ecs systems            # confirm THIS build's systems are registered + ticking
npx iwsdk ecs find --input-json '{"withComponents":["PanelUI"]}'   # confirm panels mounted now
npx iwsdk browser screenshot     # confirm what actually rendered this round
```

This is the same trap as cloning a prior game as a scaffold (it propagates stale
assets and anti-patterns): a stale "verified" claim propagates false confidence.
A screenshot and a fresh `ecs systems` are cheap; a shipped dead loop is not.

## Re-run the FULL verification gate every round

A green `vite build` is NOT verification — esbuild transpiles, it does not
type-check, and a clean bundle proves nothing about whether the scene renders,
panels mount, or input responds. The real gate is `tsc --noEmit` PLUS a rendered
screenshot, and it is a **per-build** gate. Run the complete sequence from
`references/05-verify-runtime.md` every round and every milestone — do not skip
it because "the last round passed."

The two non-negotiable gates, every round:

```bash
npx tsc --noEmit                 # REQUIRED type gate (template ships strict + noEmit)
npx iwsdk browser screenshot     # REQUIRED visual gate — capture a rendered frame
```

Then the rest of the gate (systems registered, zero console errors, panels
mounted) exactly as `05-verify-runtime.md` describes. Re-run it _before_ each
deploy, not once at the end of the project.

A specific cross-round failure mode: an app that "built and screenshotted fine
last round" can still have a **dead game loop** this round if you guessed an
engine path and cast it away.

```ts
// BAD — guesses a non-existent path, then silently runs a raw rAF loop
try {
  (world as any).ecs?.registerSystem?.(UpdateSystem);
} catch {
  requestAnimationFrame(rafLoop);
}
```

The correct call is `world.registerSystem(GameLoopSystem)`. Don't wrap engine
calls in `try/catch` that swallows and falls back; let them throw. After every
`dev up`, confirm with `npx iwsdk ecs systems` that your systems are listed — if
absent, the loop is running off-ECS and the ECS debug tools (`pause`/`step`/
`snapshot`/`query`/`find`) have nothing to inspect, because they only see
registered Systems and components, not hand-rolled rAF loops or plain JS fields.

## Recovering an unresponsive runtime

Across a long iterate loop the managed browser/bridge will eventually drop
(`socket hang up` / `connection lost` on a runtime command, or commands timing
out). That is a transport problem, NOT an XR limitation. Recover in order:

1. `npx iwsdk xr status`
2. `npx iwsdk browser logs`
3. `npx iwsdk browser reload`
4. If transport/server state is bad: `npx iwsdk dev restart`
5. Re-enter XR if needed: `npx iwsdk xr enter`

`iwsdk xr enter` failures split into two taxonomies: **app-state** (`No session
has been offered` → the app didn't call `World.create` with an `xr.offer`, or the
offer hasn't fired yet) vs **transport** (`socket hang up` / `connection lost` →
the managed browser/bridge dropped; recover with the steps above). For a
`browser screenshot` `socket hang up`, retry once (the bridge relaunches on the
next request) before reloading/restarting — and never substitute an `ecs
snapshot` for a screenshot, since counts say nothing about what rendered.

If the browser won't launch at all with `dev_browser_not_ready` +
`Executable doesn't exist at …/chromium_headless_shell-<rev>/`, that is the
Playwright revision mismatch — see `references/troubleshooting.md` and fix it
rather than falling back to `ecs`/`scene` (they relay through the same managed
browser). If it still won't launch, say so explicitly ("scene not visually
verified — browser unavailable") rather than implying it renders.

## Re-sync adapters after dependency changes

Project creation syncs MCP adapter configs for the selected AI tools after
dependency installation. When a round changes dependencies (a reinstall, a
package bump, switching tarball ↔ linked source), re-sync them so the adapter
configs match the current install:

```bash
npx iwsdk adapter sync      # write configs for all supported or selected AI tools
npx iwsdk adapter status    # check adapter state
npx iwsdk adapter prune     # remove IWSDK-managed MCP entries
```

Supported adapters: Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Codex, GitHub Copilot. (This
guide stays CLI-first regardless of adapter state — use the `npx iwsdk ...`
commands directly; do not plan around MCP tools that may not actually be wired.)

## Confirm APIs against the reference, never guess

When you extend the app in a later round and are unsure of an API, **look it up
instead of guessing a method name and casting `world`/components to `any` to
silence the resulting type error.** Guessing is what produces silent no-ops like
`(world as any).ecs?.registerSystem?.(...)` and `(world as any).render?.camera`
— those members don't exist at runtime. The reference CLI is the right tool:

```bash
npx iwsdk reference api --input-json '{"name":"World.create"}'
npx iwsdk reference systems --input-json '{}'
npx iwsdk reference components --input-json '{}'
npx iwsdk reference examples --input-json '{"api_name":"DistanceGrabbable"}'
npx iwsdk reference search --input-json '{"query":"how to create a grabbable object","limit":5}'
```

For the common surfaces you'll reach for mid-iteration — `world.*` members, the
ECS System tick, Input, Physics, Audio, PanelUI — `references/api-quick-reference.md`
is the fast lookup, and `references/cli-reference.md` has the full `iwsdk` command
tree. A TS error on a real typed member (`world.camera`, `world.registerSystem`,
`entity.object3D`) is a real bug to fix, never "a known IWSDK typing issue" to
cast away.

## Round checklist

Every iterate round / milestone, in order:

1. Make the change. Per-frame logic stays in a registered `System` (never
   `world.onUpdate` / `world.ecs.registerSystem` / raw `requestAnimationFrame`).
2. If you touched dependencies: `npx iwsdk adapter sync`.
3. `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean (filter framework noise per `05-verify-runtime.md`,
   do not dismiss real `src/` errors).
4. `npx iwsdk dev up` (recover the runtime first if it's unresponsive).
5. `npx iwsdk browser screenshot` + `npx iwsdk browser logs` (zero errors) —
   re-derived THIS round, not copied.
6. `npx iwsdk ecs systems` / `ecs find` — confirm systems tick and panels mount
   in THIS build.
7. Unsure of any API you added? `npx iwsdk reference api|systems|examples` —
   don't guess-and-cast.

item.thinking:
The screenshot shows:
- Rocket on the launch pad with nose cone, body stages, fins, nozzle - all visible
- Holodeck environment: grid floor, grid ceiling, support towers with lights
- Cyan glowing launch pad edge
- The scene renders properly

Browser logs:
- IWSDK v0.3.1 banner (though we're supposed to be on 0.4.1+... but this could just be the actual version installed)
- Multiple Three.js instances warning (common, not a blocker)
- WebGL performance warnings (SwiftShader ReadPixels stall — normal for headless)
- No actual errors

The title panel should be visible but the camera is zoomed in on the rocket. The title UI panel is positioned at z=-2 in world space, so it might be out of frame. Looking at what's already implemented—15 missions, 60 achievements, 12 skins, 8 themes, weather system, career mode, flight physics with atmospheric drag and gravity, 19 PanelUI panels, audio, particles, and trails—I'm thinking about what could elevate Round 3. A replay or ghost system to record and playback flight paths would be compelling, or expanding Mission Control functionality.

For the most impactful additions, I'm leaning toward visual stage separation where lower stages actually detach and animate away, camera shake and speed line effects during high-velocity flight, automated Mission Control callouts via toast notifications at key flight events, and orbital insertion visualization that draws the orbit ellipse when approaching target altitude.

item.text:

1. **Visual stage separation** — animate stage detachment with debris
2. **Camera shake + speed effects** at high velocity
3. **Mission control callouts** — automated toasts at key flight events
4. **Orbital insertion visualization** — draw orbit ellipse approaching target
5. **Tutorial mode** — guided first launch
6. **Expanded difficulty presets** (Easy/Normal/Hard/Expert physics)
7. **More achievements, polish**

Context checkpoint compaction summary:
## Context Checkpoint Summary

### Who is the user?
The user (Felix) is building a portfolio of VR games using IWSDK (Immersive Web SDK) deployed to GitHub Pages under the `ellyz2426` GitHub account. Games follow a "holodeck aesthetic" (neon wireframe, futuristic). An automated daily build pipeline runs two cycles (AM at midnight PT, PM at noon PT) with hourly sentinel check-ins.

### Current State
- **Portfolio:** 34 completed games across 33+ genres out of 39 attempts
- **Latest completed builds:**
  - AM #39: Neon Toss VR (ring toss carnival game) — live at `https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-toss/`
  - PM #38: Neon Blocks VR (Tetris-style) — live at `https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-blocks/`
- **Active build:** A rocket launch simulation game was manually kicked off as a subagent. First spawn errored (inference/model error), then was respawned. The user asked which phase it's currently in — this question has NOT been answered yet.

### Key Recent Changes
1. **Updated IWSDK dev skill** installed at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/` — user uploaded a new zip with a phase-based SKILL.md, 10 reference docs in `references/`, and a `scripts/preflight-checks.sh`. The old single-file skill was replaced.
2. **Skill structure** is 7 build phases: Scaffold → World+Loop → Subsystems → Spatial UI → Verify → Deploy → Iterate, each with a separate reference file.
3. The user asked for an updated cron job prompt referencing the new skill; a draft was provided but **not yet applied** to the actual cron files.
4. The PM daily build cron (`iwsdk-daily-build-pm`) was **disabled** to prevent conflicts with the manual rocket launch build. Needs to be re-enabled tomorrow.

### Infrastructure
- **Cron jobs:** AM build at midnight PT, PM build at noon PT, hourly check-ins (1am–7am for AM, 1pm–7pm for PM), summary jobs at 8:30am and 8:30pm PT
- **Status files:** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` (AM) and `current-build-status-pm.json` (PM)
- **Build log:** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` tracks all past games
- **Skills paths:** Built-in at `/home/hatch/skills/`, custom at `/home/hatch/workspace/skills/`
- **Hatch Spaces** were deployed on May 28: Neon Drift VR and VR Pong (both `local` share type via Cloudflare proxy). Space deploy skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-space-deploy/SKILL.md`.
- **GitHub:** repos under `ellyz2426`, deployed to GitHub Pages via `gh-pages` branch. Token lacks `workflow` scope.
- **Telegram notifications** sent to target `8466748050` for build summaries.

### What Needs to Be Done Next
1. **Answer the user's question**: Check on the rocket launch simulation subagent (ID: `6faef189-21f8-4ce2-a5e8-367e20023519`) — determine its current status and which build phase it's in. Use `subagents list` or check the PM status file.
2. **Apply updated cron prompts** — The user hasn't explicitly asked for this yet, but a draft was provided and the old prompts still reference nonexistent "section 4b".
3. **Re-enable PM cron** tomorrow (it was disabled for the manual build).

### Constraints & Preferences
- All game UI must use PanelUI/uikitml spatial panels — zero HTML DOM overlays (invisible in XR)
- XR controller support mandatory
- Dual runtime (VR + browser-first)
- Holodeck aesthetic throughout
- Games deployed to GitHub Pages under `ellyz2426`
- Build journals (JSONL) pushed to `.agent/journal/` in each game repo
- User receives Telegram notifications for completed builds

name: iwsdk-dev
description: >-
  MUST read BEFORE building VR/MR or browser-first 3D apps with IWSDK (Immersive
  Web SDK / @iwsdk/core). A phase-based build guide — scaffold → world+loop →
  subsystems → spatial UI → verify → deploy → iterate — that keeps the
  non-negotiable correctness rules always in view and loads a focused reference
  per phase. Use when scaffolding, building, debugging, or deploying WebXR/3D
  apps, when working with PanelUI/uikitml, ECS systems, physics, the iwsdk CLI,
  or when the user mentions iwsdk, IWSDK, @iwsdk, WebXR, or `npx iwsdk`.
---

# IWSDK Development Guide

Targets the IWSDK **0.4.x** line · [iwsdk.dev](https://iwsdk.dev) · built on Three.js (super-three) + an ECS.

This skill is phase-based. Find the phase you're in below, read its terse highlights here,
and **load the one matching `references/` file** for depth. The correctness rules in the next
section are always in force, in every phase.

## CLI-first operating model

`npx iwsdk ...` is the primary control surface in cloud/headless harnesses — for setup, dev
server, screenshots, ECS/XR inspection, and **reference lookup**. Prefer it over MCP tools,
which are often unwired in cloud harnesses; do not rewrite CLI examples into MCP-only flows.

**Ground your API knowledge in the reference CLI instead of guessing.** `@iwsdk/reference` is a
semantic search over the real SDK (`reference search` / `api` / `systems` / `components` /
`examples`). It needs a **one-time warmup** that downloads a corpus + model into cache:

```bash
npx iwsdk reference warmup     # one-time, ~120MB into cache; run it during scaffold (Phase 0)
npx iwsdk reference status      # confirm ready
npx iwsdk reference api    --input-json '{"name":"World.create"}'
npx iwsdk reference search --input-json '{"query":"how to register a per-frame system","limit":5}'
```

> **If a reference command errors as not-ready / cache-miss / corpus-missing, RUN
> `npx iwsdk reference warmup` and retry — do NOT give up and guess the API.** Warmup is cheap
> and pays for itself: it is your ground truth for any "what's the real method/component?"
> question and the antidote to the `(world as any)` / wrong-API guessing that breaks builds.
> (See `references/cli-reference.md` for warmup recovery, and `references/api-quick-reference.md`
> for the typed surface at a glance.)

## The 7 build phases

| Phase                       | You are…                                     | Load                                   |
| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| 0 · Scaffold & First Render | creating the app + getting a frame to render | `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md` |
| 1 · World & the Game Loop   | calling `World.create`, standing up the loop | `references/02-world-and-loop.md`      |
| 2 · Gameplay Subsystems     | three imports, physics, audio, input, coords | `references/03-subsystems.md`          |
| 3 · Spatial UI (PanelUI)    | authoring + wiring uikitml panels            | `references/04-spatial-ui.md`          |
| 4 · Verify (continuous)     | proving it renders, ticks, responds          | `references/05-verify-runtime.md`      |
| 5 · Build & Deploy          | deploying — Hatch Space (default), GH Pages opt-in | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md`    |
| 6 · Iterate                 | re-verifying + extending across rounds       | `references/07-iterate.md`             |

## Non-negotiable correctness rules (always in force)

These six prevent the most expensive failures. Each is _decided early but surfaces late_, so
internalize them up front. Terse here; full treatment in the tagged reference.

1. **Import every Three.js class from `@iwsdk/core`, never from `three`.** The runtime is
   super-three; importing from `three` (or `import * as THREE`) drifts types and `instanceof`.
   IWSDK apps are ESM-only — no `require`. _[Phase 2 → 03-subsystems.md]_
2. **Per-frame logic MUST be an ECS System** — `class X extends createSystem({...})` with
   `update(delta, time)`, registered via `world.registerSystem(X)`. `world.onUpdate`,
   `world.ecs.registerSystem`, `renderer.setAnimationLoop`, and raw `requestAnimationFrame`
   do **not** tick the loop and silently ship a dead game. _[Phase 1 → 02-world-and-loop.md]_
3. **`World` is fully typed — never `(world as any)`.** `world.camera`, `world.scene`,
   `world.renderer`, `world.input`, `world.player`, `world.registerSystem` all exist. A TS error
   here is a real bug, not a framework quirk. _[Phase 1 → 02-world-and-loop.md]_
4. **PanelUI loads async, is uikit (not DOM), and needs relative config paths.** Wire handlers
   in the query `'qualify'` event (not `setTimeout`); mutate with `setProperties` (not
   `textContent`/`onclick`); author `config: './ui/x.json'` relative (root-absolute breaks deploy: GitHub 404s, and the Space daemon serves only `assets/`).
   _[Phase 3 author → 04-spatial-ui.md · Phase 5 bites → 06-build-and-deploy.md]_
5. **`tsc --noEmit` + a rendered screenshot is the real verification gate.** `vite build` does
   not type-check; a green build is **not** verification. Re-run the gate every milestone.
   _[Phase 4 → 05-verify-runtime.md]_
6. **Use the real subsystem APIs, not hand-rolled substitutes.** Physics needs `PhysicsShape` +
   `PhysicsBody` (don't write an integrator); audio uses `AudioSource` / `AudioUtils` (not raw
   Web Audio). _[Phase 2 → 03-subsystems.md]_

When unsure of any API, confirm it with `npx iwsdk reference api|systems|components` (warm it up
first) rather than casting to `any`.

## Phase highlights

### Phase 0 — Scaffold & First Render → `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md`

- Check `node -v` ≥ 20.19 **before** scaffolding (Vite 7 throws `crypto.hash is not a function`
  on Node 18; `dev:runtime` spawns a child `vite` that reverts to the default Node — make 20/22
  the persistent default, don't drop it in `/tmp`).
- Scaffold **fresh** with `npx @iwsdk/create@latest … --no-metaspatial`; infer flags from the
  request. Never clone-and-rename a prior game (drags stale assets/config/anti-patterns).
- `npm run dev` == `npx iwsdk dev up`; read `localUrl` (`https://127.0.0.1:8081/`) from
  `dev status`. SwiftShader auto-detects in 0.4.x — no `node_modules` patching.
- **Warm up the reference CLI now:** `npx iwsdk reference warmup` (so API lookups work later).
- Behind a proxy / on Node-18 / on a Playwright `chromium_headless_shell-1208` mismatch → load
  the reference.

### Phase 1 — World & the Game Loop → `references/02-world-and-loop.md`

- `await World.create(container, {...})`; options are typed (don't cast). Rules **2 and 3** live
  here — this is the costly correctness fork.
- After `World.create`, register systems: `world.registerSystem(GameSystem)`. The loop is
  already running; never start your own.

### Phase 2 — Gameplay Subsystems → `references/03-subsystems.md`

- Three imports (rule 1), physics components (rule 6), `AudioSource`/`AudioUtils` (rule 6),
  XR controller + keyboard input, coordinates. One file, jump via its `## Contents`.
- Input gamepad APIs take **string ids** (`InputComponent.Trigger`) and `getAxesValues` returns
  `{x,y}` — a number id or array index silently returns `undefined`.

### Phase 3 — Spatial UI (PanelUI / uikitml) → `references/04-spatial-ui.md`

- Author `ui/*.uikitml`; the `compileUIKit()` vite plugin emits `public/ui/*.json` (no CLI to
  compile). Reference the **compiled** path, **relative** (rule 4).
- Loads async → wire in the `'qualify'` event; elements are uikit → `setProperties`. ASCII-only
  glyphs; no multi-value `padding`/`margin` shorthand.

### Phase 4 — Verify in the Live Runtime → `references/05-verify-runtime.md`

- Rule 5. `npx tsc --noEmit` → `dev up` → `browser screenshot` → `browser logs` (zero errors) →
  `ecs systems` (your systems are registered = the loop is real). Re-run every milestone.
- `ecs pause`/`step` to freeze a play frame for a stable screenshot.

### Phase 5 — Build & Deploy → `references/06-build-and-deploy.md`

- `npm run build`, then **deploy as a Hatch Space by default**: `create_space` (registers it) →
  rewrite `index.html` (both `data-generated-space-root=""` + `data-generated-space-bootstrap=""`
  markers, drop the SDK bundle) → move every asset under `assets/` → patch runtime paths to
  `./assets/…` → `share share-space --slug <slug>`. Rebuild before publishing (stale-artifact trap).
- **GitHub Pages is opt-in** — only when the user explicitly asks (or needs VM-independent
  hosting, since a `local` Space needs the VM running): `npm run build && npx gh-pages -d dist`.
- The #1 deploy break: the Space daemon serves **only `assets/`**, so any runtime-fetched path
  (PanelUI `config`, gltf/audio/textures) 401s unless it resolves under `./assets/` — and the same
  root-absolute `config: '/ui/…'` 404s on a GitHub subpath. Author configs relative.

### Phase 6 — Iterate → `references/07-iterate.md`

- Re-run the full Phase 4 gate **every** round against _this_ build; never reuse a prior round's
  counts/claims. Recover an unresponsive runtime; re-sync adapters after dependency changes.

## When to load references (route by what you're doing)

| If you're… / you hit…                                                  | Load                                |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| "nothing is ticking / `ecs systems` is empty / dead loop"              | `references/02-world-and-loop.md`   |
| "a panel won't appear or bind"                                         | `references/04-spatial-ui.md`       |
| "401/404 on panels or assets **after deploy**" (Space or Pages)        | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md` |
| "build passed — is it done?"                                           | `references/05-verify-runtime.md`   |
| confirming a real member/signature (instead of casting to `any`)       | `references/api-quick-reference.md` |
| any `iwsdk` command, warmup recovery, or "does this subcommand exist?" | `references/cli-reference.md`       |
| a raw error string you can't place                                     | `references/troubleshooting.md`     |

Run the bundled gates with `bash scripts/preflight-checks.sh` (non-ASCII glyphs, Yoga shorthand,
root-absolute UI configs, CJS idioms, uikitml→json coverage) before deploying.

## Top friction points (most-wasteful mistakes, by phase)

1. Shipping a **no-op game loop** via raw rAF / `world.onUpdate` / `world.ecs.registerSystem` — _P1_.
2. Reinventing **PanelUI readiness** with `setTimeout` instead of the `'qualify'` event — _P3_.
3. **Assets the Space daemon won't serve** (everything fetched at runtime must live under `assets/`) / root-absolute UI config that 404s on a GitHub subpath — _P3/P5_.
4. Treating a green `vite build` as **verification** (it doesn't type-check) — _P4_.
5. Casting `(world as any)` / guessing APIs instead of warming up the **reference CLI** — _all phases_.
6. Building on **Node 18** (Vite 7 `crypto.hash`) or cloning a prior game as a scaffold — _P0_.

// ============================================================================
// TYPES & CONSTANTS
// ============================================================================

type GameState = 'title' | 'config' | 'preflight' | 'countdown' | 'flying' | 'orbit' | 'gameover' | 'achievements' | 'stats' | 'settings' | 'help' | 'skins' | 'modes' | 'difficulty' | 'weather' | 'career' | 'leaderboard';

interface RocketConfig {
  stages: number; // 2 or 3
  payload: string; // 'satellite' | 'probe' | 'crew' | 'station'
  fuelType: string; // 'standard' | 'high-thrust' | 'efficient'
}

interface FlightData {
  altitude: number; // meters
  velocity: number; // m/s
  acceleration: number; // m/s^2
  fuel: number; // 0-100%
  angle: number; // degrees from vertical
  stage: number; // current stage
  maxStages: number;
  throttle: number; // 0-100%
  apoapsis: number; // target orbit altitude
  periapsis: number; // current min orbit alt
  missionTime: number;
  maxAltitude: number;
  maxVelocity: number;
  score: number;
  stageSeparations: number;
  orbitalVelocity: number; // m/s needed for orbit
  dragCoeff: number;
  thrust: number;
  mass: number;
  gravity: number;
}

interface Mission {
  name: string;
  target: number; // target altitude km
  payload: string;
  difficulty: string;
  description: string;
}

interface Achievement {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  desc: string;
  unlocked: boolean;
}

interface RocketSkin {
  name: string;
  body: string;
  accent: string;
  flame: string;
  unlockCondition: string;
  unlocked: boolean;
}

interface ArenaTheme {
  name: string;
  grid: string;
  accent: string;
  bg: string;
  fog: string;
  wall: string;
  sky: string;
  glow: string;
}

interface WeatherCondition {
  name: string;
  windSpeed: number;
  turbulence: number;
  headwind: number;
  visibility: number;
}

interface AltitudeMilestone {
  altitude: number;
  name: string;
}

interface LeaderboardEntry {
  mission: string;
  score: number;
  altitude: number;
  time: number;
  date: string;
}
